close_reason: Positive observation: eager validation is correct design
description: lib.rs:144-146 calls make_transport(&server) at from_uri() time so a syntactically invalid ?server= surfaces as InvalidUri (permanent) before any get/put/sign. This means callers can fail fast on misconfiguration at startup rather than at first use, and they cannot confuse InvalidUri (your URI is wrong) with Unavailable (server is down). A future reviewer might suggest 'simplify by validating lazily everywhere' — defend the eager check; the separation of permanent vs transient is the contract callers rely on.
title: Eager ?server= validation at from_uri() separates permanent config errors from transient runtime errors
